_note: ILE RPG 仕様書プロンプター定義の英訳。日本語版(prompter/rpg/ile/ja)の表示文字を訳したもの。桁・欄名・選択肢の値といった事実は日本語版が正で、ここには含めない（generate-rpg-spec-definitions.mjs が構造をそのまま引き継ぐ）。原典の英文は自由形式の構文説明が混ざり欄の説明として長すぎるため、日本語版の簡潔な説明を訳している。

C-NEW.help: Write extended-factor-2 calculations (EVAL, IF, and so on) on a C-NEW line.

CTLLEVEL.help: L1-L9, LR, SR, and so on.

OPCODE.help: An extended-factor-2 operation code such as EVAL or IF.

COND.help: The expression written in place of factor 2.

C-SPEC.help: Write traditional-format calculations on a C-SPEC line.

CTLLEVEL.help: L1-L9, LR, SR, AN/OR, and so on.

INDICATORS.help: Conditioning indicators. Prefix with N to negate.

OPCODE.help: The operation to perform, such as CALL, EVAL, or IF.

FACTOR2.help: The right operand: a literal, a number, or a field name.

RESULT.help: The field that receives the result of the operation.

FIELDLEN.help: The length to use when the result field is defined here.

DECPOS.help: Decimal positions when the result field is numeric.

RESULTIND.help: Indicators set by the operation (three pairs).

D-SPEC.help: Define fields and data structures on a D-SPEC line.

NAME.help: Up to 14 characters. Identifies the data structure or variable.

EFLAG.help: Enter E for an externally described field.

SU.help: Enter S for signed or U for unsigned.

DECLTYPE.help: The kind of definition: DS, S, C, PR, PI, and so on.

FROM.help: The starting position within a data structure.

LEN.help: The length. Numeric entries are stored right-aligned.

INTTYPE.help: The field type: A, P, S, B, I, F, D, T, or Z.

DEC.help: Decimal positions for a numeric field.

KEYWORDS.help: Keywords for the definition, in positions 44-80.

F-SPEC.help: Declare the files the program uses (position 6 = F). Positions follow the ILE RPG IV fixed-format layout; positions 44-80 hold keywords.

FILENAME.help: The file name, in positions 7-16 (up to 10 characters).

FILETYPE.help: How the file is used. I = input only, O = output only, U = update, C = combined.

FILEDESG.help: F = full procedural. Blank = demand file.

ENDFILE.help: E = end the program when this file reaches end of file. Blank = do not end.

FILEADD.help: A = records may be added to the file. Blank = no additions.

FILEFMT.help: E = externally described. F = program described.

RECLEN.help: Record length of a program-described file. Normally blank when externally described.

KEYLEN.description: Length of key or record address
KEYLEN.help: Key length of a program-described file. Blank when externally described.

RECADDR.help: The record address type. Usually K for a keyed file.

FILEORG.help: The file organization. Enter K for keyed access.

DEVICE.help: The device type. DISK = database, PRINTER = printer, WORKSTN = display, SPECIAL = special, SEQ = sequential.

KEYWORDS.help: Keywords for the file definition. For example USROPN, RENAME(external:internal), SFILE(record:rrn), INFDS(data-structure), COMMIT, KEYED.

H-SPEC.help: Give program-wide control information as keywords (position 6 = H). Keywords are written in positions 7-80; they are not tied to fixed positions.

DATFMT.help: The default date format for the program.

DATEDIT.help: The edit format for numeric dates: a format (*YMD, *MDY, *DMY, *JUL) plus a separator (/ - . , or blank). For example *YMD/.

DFTACTGRP.help: Whether the program runs in the default activation group.

ACTGRP.help: The activation group: *NEW, *CALLER, *STGMDL, or a group name. Applies when DFTACTGRP(*NO) is specified.

OPTIMIZE.help: The optimization level used at compile time.

DEBUG.help: Enables debugging aids. For example *YES, *NO, or *CONSTANTS *DUMP.

OPTION.help: Compile options; more than one may be given. For example *NODEBUGIO *SRCSTMT *NOUNREF.

DECEDIT.help: The decimal point and digit separator. For example '0,', '.', or ','.

BNDDIR.help: The binding directory to use. For example 'MYLIB/MYBNDDIR'.

COPYRIGHT.help: Copyright text stored in the object.

I-SPEC-FLD-EXT.help: Field description entries for an externally described file. Adds RPG function to a field of the external description.

EXTFIELD.help: The name of the field as defined in the external description.

FIELDNAME.help: The name used inside the program, when the field is renamed.

CTLLEVEL.help: L1-L9. Marks the field that causes a control break.

MATCHFLD.help: M1-M9. Used to match records across files.

FLDIND.help: Indicators set when the field is plus, minus, zero, or blank.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

I-SPEC-FLD-PGM.help: Field description entries for a program-described file. Defines the fields within a record.

DTSEP.help: The separator used in date and time fields.

DATAFMT.help: The data format of the field.

FIELDPOS.help: The starting and ending positions within the record.

DECPOS.help: Decimal positions of a numeric field.

FIELDNAME.help: The name used for the field inside the program.

CTLLEVEL.help: L1-L9. Marks the field that causes a control break.

MATCHFLD.help: M1-M9. Used to match records across files.

FLDRECREL.help: The indicator that ties the field to a particular record.

FLDIND.help: Indicators set when the field is plus, minus, zero, or blank.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

I-SPEC-REC-EXT.help: Record identification entries for an externally described file (positions 7-16 and 21-22). Identifies the external record format that RPG function is added to.

RCDNAME.help: The name of the external record format being extended.

RECIDIND.help: The indicator set on when the record is read.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

I-SPEC-REC-PGM.help: Record identification entries for a program-described file (positions 7-46). Describes how an input record relates to the other records in the file.

FILENAME.help: The name of the file the input record belongs to.

SEQUENCE.help: The sequence of the record. An alphabetic entry means the sequence is not checked.

NUMBER.help: 1 = once within the sequence, N = more than once.

OPTION.help: O = this record type is optional.

RECIDIND.help: The indicator set on when the record is read.

RECIDCODE.help: The codes that identify the record: three sets of position, NOT, code part, and character.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

O-SPEC-FLD-EXT.description: Output specification, field description and control (externally described)
O-SPEC-FLD-EXT.help: Field description and control entries for an externally described file.

OUTIND.help: Indicators that condition output of this field (three sets).

FIELDNAME.help: The name of the field to write.

BLANKAFT.help: B = set the field to blanks or zeros after it is written.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

O-SPEC-FLD-PGM.description: Output specification, field description and control (program described)
O-SPEC-FLD-PGM.help: Field description and control entries for a program-described file.

OUTIND.help: Indicators that condition output of this field (three sets).

FIELDNAME.help: The name of the field to write.

EDITCODE.help: The edit code applied to a numeric field.

BLANKAFT.help: B = set the field to blanks or zeros after it is written.

ENDPOS.help: The ending position within the record.

DATAFMT.help: The data format of a date or time field.

CONSTANT.help: A constant, an edit word, data attributes, or a format name.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

O-SPEC-REC-EXT.description: Output specification, record identification and control (externally described)
O-SPEC-REC-EXT.help: Record identification and control entries for an externally described file (positions 7-39).

RCDNAME.help: The name of the external record format to write to.

OUTTYPE.help: H = heading, D = detail, T = total, E = exception.

ADDDEL.help: ADD = add the record, DEL = delete it.

OUTIND.help: Indicators that condition the output (three sets).

EXCEPTNAME.help: The name referred to by an EXCEPT operation.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

O-SPEC-REC-PGM.description: Output specification, record identification and control (program described)
O-SPEC-REC-PGM.help: Record identification and control entries for a program-described file (positions 7-51).

FILENAME.help: The name of the file to write to.

OUTTYPE.help: H = heading, D = detail, T = total, E = exception.

OUTIND.help: Indicators that condition the output (three sets).

EXCEPTNAME.help: The name referred to by an EXCEPT operation.

SKIPBEF.help: The line to skip to before printing.

SKIPAFT.help: The line to skip to after printing.

COMMENT.help: The comment area of the specification (positions 81-100).

OVFADD.description: Fetch overflow / record addition or deletion
OVFADD.help: For a printer file, position 18 holds F (fetch overflow) or R (release). For a database file, positions 18-20 hold ADD or DEL. Only one of the two is written.

P-SPEC.help: Mark the beginning and end of a subprocedure (position 6 = P). The begin line (B) pairs with a prototype on a D specification. Positions follow ILE RPG IV; positions 44-80 hold keywords.

PROCNAME.help: The subprocedure name (up to 15 characters). Use the same name on the begin (B) and end (E) lines.

BEGINEND.help: B = beginning of the procedure, E = end of the procedure.

KEYWORDS.help: Keywords for the procedure definition, used on the begin line. For example EXPORT, or EXTPROC('name') to name the external procedure.
